headline: Knockout drama continues — France and Spain booked for Dallas; England await Argentina or Switzerland.

snapshot.format: 48 teams · 12 groups of 4 · Round of 32 → Round of 16 → Quarter-finals → Semi-finals → Final
snapshot.opening_match_note: First finals with three host nations and a Round of 32 knockout round

firsts: First World Cup co-hosted by three nations
First World Cup with a Round of 32 stage

hosts_detail.note: First time Canada hosts men's World Cup matches
Mexico becomes the first nation to host World Cup matches in three different editions (1970, 1986, 2026)
Final staged in the New York New Jersey metro area

fun_facts.title: Triple hosts
fun_facts.fact: FIFA World Cup 2026 is the first men's World Cup co-hosted by three countries: Canada, Mexico, and the United States.

fun_facts.title: Mexico's historic three
fun_facts.fact: Mexico is the first nation to stage World Cup matches across three different tournaments (1970, 1986, and 2026).

fun_facts.title: Expanded field
fun_facts.fact: The 2026 finals expanded to 48 teams in 12 groups of four — the largest World Cup field in history.

fun_facts.title: New knockout round
fun_facts.fact: 2026 introduced a Round of 32 between the group stage and the Round of 16 for the first time at a men's World Cup.

fun_facts.title: Final venue
fun_facts.fact: The 2026 World Cup Final is scheduled for 19 July 2026 at New York New Jersey Stadium.

fun_facts.title: Semi-final: France vs Spain
fun_facts.fact: France meet Spain in a 2026 World Cup semi-final on 14 July at Dallas Stadium.

fun_facts.title: England's semi path
fun_facts.fact: England reach a 2026 semi-final in Atlanta after beating Norway 2–1 in the quarter-finals; their opponent is Argentina or Switzerland.

fun_facts.title: France edge Morocco
fun_facts.fact: France beat Morocco 2–0 in a 2026 quarter-final at Boston Stadium.

fun_facts.title: Spain past Belgium
fun_facts.fact: Spain defeated Belgium 2–1 in the 2026 quarter-finals at Los Angeles Stadium.

fun_facts.title: England past Haaland's Norway
fun_facts.fact: England beat Norway 2–1 in a 2026 quarter-final at Miami Stadium despite Erling Haaland's seven-goal Golden Boot chase.

fun_facts.title: Host Canada exit
fun_facts.fact: Co-host Canada were eliminated 0–3 by Morocco in the Round of 16 at Houston Stadium.

fun_facts.title: Golden Boot deadlock
fun_facts.fact: As of mid-July 2026, Kylian Mbappé and Lionel Messi are level on 8 goals for the Golden Boot; Mbappé leads on assists (3 vs 1).

fun_facts.title: Haaland on seven
fun_facts.fact: Erling Haaland has 7 goals for Norway at World Cup 2026, third in the Golden Boot race behind Mbappé and Messi.

fun_facts.title: Kane among the scorers
fun_facts.fact: Harry Kane has 6 goals for England at World Cup 2026, fourth in the Golden Boot standings.

fun_facts.title: Goals flowing
fun_facts.fact: Through the knockout path into the semis, World Cup 2026 is averaging about 2.9 goals per match across an estimated 280 tournament goals.

fun_facts.title: Sixteen host cities
fun_facts.fact: Matches at World Cup 2026 are staged across 16 host cities in Canada, Mexico, and the United States.

fun_facts.title: Classic powers return
fun_facts.fact: By the semi-final stage of 2026, the remaining paths feature traditional powers France, Spain, and England, with Argentina or Switzerland still alive on the other side of the bracket.

fun_facts.title: Creators on five
fun_facts.fact: Ousmane Dembélé and Jude Bellingham each have 5 goals at World Cup 2026, tied fifth in the Golden Boot race.

fun_facts.title: Bronze in Miami
fun_facts.fact: The 2026 third-place match is scheduled for 18 July at Miami Stadium between the semi-final runners-up.

fun_facts.title: Business end of a new format
fun_facts.fact: The expanded 48-team World Cup reached the business end with hosts still relevant early, then classic European and South American powers taking over the semi-final picture.

tournament_so_far.story: The expanded 48-team World Cup reached the business end with hosts still in the conversation early, then classic powers taking over. France and Spain meet in one semi; England’s path depends on Argentina’s quarter-final against Switzerland.
highlights: First 48-team finals — 12 groups, new Round of 32 round
Tri-nation hosts: Canada, Mexico, United States
Golden Boot race tight: Mbappé and Messi level on 8 goals (assists separate them)
Final set for 19 July at New York New Jersey Stadium
Mexico becomes first nation to host World Cup matches in three editions
Co-host Canada exited in the Round of 16 to Morocco

sources: FIFA knockout bracket & Golden Boot coverage (July 2026)
disclaimer: Arena64 snapshot for agent knowledge & coaches — not an official FIFA feed. Figures approximate as of the listed update date.
